action.content:
You are playing a text adventure game. I will describe what you can perceive in the game. You write the single action you want to take in the game starting with >. Only reply with an action.
For example:
> open cupboard

You can have up to two objects in your inventory at the same time.

Your goal for this game is to brew a potion following the potion recipe.

Once you have achieved your goal, write "> done" to end the game.

You are in a bedroom now. There are a pillow, a book, a bed, a wardrobe and a potion recipe. The pillow is on the floor. The book is on the bed. The wardrobe is closed. The potion recipe is on the bed. There is a passage to a reading room here.
action.label: context

action.content:
You read the potion recipe:
That odd potion
Ingredients: Ectoplasm, spiderweb, nightshade, lily of the valley.
1. Pour ectoplasm into your cauldron.
2. Add the lily of the valley into your cauldron.
3. Put the spiderweb into your cauldron.
4. Wiggle a zulpowand at your cauldron.
5. Wiggle a ice wand on your cauldron.
6. Put the nightshade into your cauldron.
action.label: context

action.content: You are in a reading room now. There are a shelf, a side table, a couch, a chair and a table. There are passages to a garden and a conservatory here.
action.label: context

action.content: You are in a garden now. There are a lily of the valley, a bucket of water, a nightshade and a garden bench. The lily of the valley is on the floor. The bucket of water is on the floor. The nightshade is on the floor. There are passages to a storage room and a outhouse here.
action.label: context

action.content: You take the lily of the valley. In your inventory you have a lily of the valley.
action.label: context

action.content: You take the nightshade. In your inventory you have a nightshade and a lily of the valley.
action.label: context
